scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text: B) events — both PRs explicitly alter the `events` table: Alan adds `webhook_status`/`delivery_attempts`, and Sarah adds `delivery_state`/`processor_version`.

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text:
C) Sarah — she only works Monday, Wednesday, Friday.

Thursday March 13 is not one of Sarah’s working days, and the team notes also say she’s off that day. Alan’s dentist appointment is on Wednesday, not Thursday.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B) SAML SSO — Josh told Acme it would come after the auth migration and expected it by end of Q1, but Alan explicitly said SAML was not in this phase and would add 3 weeks.

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B) The new flow removed 4 steps, including **first milestone**, which was the old activation trigger. So “completion” now means something different, making 60% vs 85% not apples-to-apples.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3 was created for load testing but was never actually used for load testing, per the infrastructure notes.

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B) All three were in migration batch-7, run on February 25. The tickets for Pinnacle, Waverly, and Redstone each list the same last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: C — Only the webhook payload format remains open. The design doc summary says cursor vs offset pagination has consensus: Sarah recommends cursor and Alan agrees. The only debated open question left is nested vs flat webhook payload.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B) src/billing/mapping.rs — Alan calls it the “rosetta stone file,” and it maps between plan_id, tier_id, and subscription.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B) CSV only — Josh’s research says all 3 prospects confirmed CSV is sufficient, no one specifically needs PDF, and Excel came from sales rather than customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — There are **0 edge case tests** and **7 end-to-end tests**. The workspace says edge case tests are **0 (0%)** and end-to-end tests are **7 (2%)**; the 4 weeks of green CI alongside 6 customer bugs also suggests the suite is missing boundary-condition coverage.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B) Long-lived JWT tokens with bearer auth — Sarah’s synthesis was that you can keep the developer experience simple like API keys while still getting JWT benefits like rotation and revocation.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The gap is **no delivery confirmation mechanism**. A webhook can get a 200 response even if the customer doesn’t actually process it, so the failure can stay invisible until much later, when things like invoice reconciliation break.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B) Takeshi at NovaTech in Tokyo — he was explicitly offered to review the proposal from an API consumer perspective and hasn’t been consulted yet.

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C) About 2.75 hours per week — the workspace total explicitly says ~2.75 hours/week, based on 1h hardcoded URLs + 45m retry logic + 1h generic error messages, plus missed bugs from the skipped test.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text:
C) 2 prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc); Sarah's existing webhook signature module means the change is localized — about half a day.

The workspace notes two specific asks about Ed25519 in the last two weeks, and says Sarah already has a webhook signature module, so adding a new algorithm should be localized there.

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C) Feature 5 — it breaks even at 5 features and is net positive from feature 6 onward. The workspace math shows: N=5 is 25 days both ways, and N=6 is 27 days with refactor vs 30 days without.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The root cause is a spec ambiguity, not a backend or data bug. The dashboard is showing the billing period start (Feb 1), while Acme expects the original signup/subscription date (Jan 15). Workspace notes say it’s a 1-line UI change.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text:
B) Annual billing requires proration, refund, and dunning changes that would conflict with the March 31 billing migration launch.

The workspace shows Billing Migration Phase 2 is targeted for March 31 and is already at risk because 847 subscriptions need reconciliation, with no buffer. Adding annual billing would introduce more billing work into the same critical path.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B) 12 minutes detection; root cause was a process gap — no testing against production-scale data with historical records. The incident log says Sarah’s monitoring fired at 09:12 after the 09:00 start, and the “what went wrong” section explicitly calls out inadequate testing against prod-scale data with legacy records.

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C) 95% utilization at baseline; during typical 1.5-2x spikes the combined 143-190 req/sec exceeds the 100 limit, causing 429 errors.
